approach: one spec at a time; the retired corpus is reference only and yielded nothing here
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the implementation, never evidence of current behavior
prefer an additive companion over rewriting a frozen scenario wherever the defect allows it
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: medium — three frozen scenarios are rewritten under a granted clearance, in the suite that governs how every other skill in this plugin is authored
reversibility: high — an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any other node
novelty: low — no new capability; the suite is corrected against an implementation that already moved
confidence: high — the contradiction was verified against the implementation text and the commit that caused it, not inferred from a report

why: RATIFIED LIVE by the owner. Scope: three frozen scenarios in define-skill's suite may be rewritten. ONE is mandatory — a scenario requiring the skill to co-produce an eval suite carrying one case per frozen scenario, which the implementation deliberately stopped doing when the frozen suite itself became the verification; the reversal is recorded in the history and the implementation states the opposite in its own opening paragraph, so a conformant implementation FAILS that frozen scenario today. It is rewritten to assert the current contract rather than deleted, keeping the branch covered and preserving its pair with the standalone-mode scenario. TWO are structural and were added to the same ask at the owner's direction: one packs several independent assertions into a single boolean so a failure cannot be attributed, and one is double-barreled with a first clause a do-nothing implementation satisfies. Neither of those two penalizes correct behavior. No other frozen scenario is touched; every remaining repair is additive and self-clears.

RATIFIED LIVE by the owner, second ask on this node, scoped to ONE frozen scenario: the report scenario that points at the eval loop gains an entry qualifier so it applies to the standalone entry only. Content otherwise untouched. Necessary because the first ask's fix RELOCATED its blocker rather than removing it: adding a scenario for the escaped entry produced a pair sharing a byte-identical When whose Givens CROSS on orthogonal axes — entry mode against artifact property — so neither entails the other, no intended winner is derivable, and an ordinary escaped run satisfies both while the two Thens demand opposite verdicts. The conductor had relied on specialization, which requires the specific Given to ENTAIL the general one; naming more conditions is not the same as being narrower, and adding a scenario cannot resolve a crossing pair. The node spec's own use-case rows are corrected in the same change and need no clearance, since spec prose is kept in sync and never frozen: one row is entry-blind and is the root cause the suite inherited, and one still describes the eval-suite behavior the implementation removed, making it the inverse of the scenario the first clearance corrected.

Built define-skill's suite up against the implementation, using the retired corpus as reference only. The corpus yielded ZERO new scenarios: seventeen of nineteen cases were already covered and two asserted rules the implementation had reversed, so the whole value was re-reading the frozen suite against the current SKILL.md. That reading found what a corpus migration would have cemented: a frozen scenario demanding the skill co-produce an eval suite the implementation deliberately stopped writing (so a conformant build FAILED it), a name-and-description scenario packing several assertions into one boolean, a double-barreled eval-file scenario, an entire uncovered entry point (escaped), and after the escaped fix a relocated contradiction on the report axis. Corrected under two recorded owner clearances scoped to exactly three rewritten scenarios and one narrowed Given; the gherkin-cli structural diff confirms removed=3 and modified=1 held across four grill rounds with no creep. Eight further repairs were additive and self-clearing: positive companions for do-nothing-passing negatives, a HIGH-band audit scenario, a de-memorized gate-role scenario that kills the blocklist implementation the SKILL.md's own reject-list invites, an impl-producer report scenario, and a standalone-partial report scenario closing the last entry/artifact state. Four cold spec-judge rounds, each closing a real defect with no regression surviving a fix; the final round returned ALIGNED with the entry-mode partition verified total and non-overlapping, and the one residual it named (standalone non-triggering report) was then closed additively per its own prescription. All mechanical checks green, no open markers. Root project status and its human ratification untouched: the suite was corrected to match an implementation that already moved.
